title: phyllopodium lupuliforme
intro: Phyllopodium lupuliforme is a flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the hopflower or lupulin flower. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as relieving muscle cramps, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Phyllopodium lupuliforme is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
